mnemonics: PearSON Teaches CORRELation: The 'son' reminds you it's about the relationship (correlation) between variables, often taught in Pearson Education materials.
keyPoints: Measures the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two continuous variables.
The correlation coefficient, r, ranges from -1 (perfect negative) to +1 (perfect positive).
It is a foundational concept for understanding more complex statistical modeling covered in ANOVA and experimental design.
commonMistakes: Confusing correlation with causation; a high correlation does not mean one variable causes the other.
Assuming a linear relationship is appropriate without checking the data for non-linear patterns first.
Using Pearson's r when the data is not continuous or normally distributed, which can lead to inaccurate results.
